What should I do if I have charges from Netflix but don’t have an account? If you never signed up for Netflix or suspect someone is using your payment information for a Netflix account without your consent, please contact us immediately.

What if I'm still being charged on an expired card? Your bank may have automatically updated your credit or debit card information when a new card was issued. This allows your card to continue being charged, even if it’s expired. You can remove or update your payment information at any time from your Account page.
Why was I charged a different amount than expected? See why your Netflix price changed.
What if I was charged after canceling my Netflix account? See Charged after canceling Netflix.
Why was I charged multiple times by Netflix? See Charged twice by Netflix.
Why was I charged twice by Netflix? There are a couple of different reasons you may see more than one charge for Netflix on your bill:

What should I do if I recently added a payment method to my account and see multiple charges? If you recently added a payment method and see more than one charge on the same day, one of the charges is likely an authorization hold. Authorization holds are used to check that a payment method is valid. Netflix never collects the authorization amount. It’ll be removed automatically, usually in 8 days or less. Check with your bank to confirm when the funds will be released back to your available balance.

What if I recently changed my plan or purchased an extra member slot and see multiple charges? If you recently changed to a higher-priced plan, the plan change takes effect immediately so you can enjoy all of the added features. Your billing date may change based on the remaining balance of your last payment, or you may be charged a prorated amount and see multiple charges in the same month.
What should I do if none of the reasons above apply and I see multiple charges from Netflix? If none of the reasons above apply and you see more than one Netflix charge, please contact us.

Why am I being charged after canceling Netflix? If your account was canceled but you’re still seeing a charge, you or someone who was using your account may have accidentally restarted it. If you can’t complete the steps below, please contact us.

What should I do if I want to stop being charged after canceling Netflix? To stop being charged, sign in to netflix.com and follow these steps: 1. Go to netflix.com/cancelplan. 2. Select Finish Cancellation. Your account will close at the end of your current billing cycle, and you won’t be charged again.

How do I prevent someone else from restarting my Netflix account after I canceled it? Change your password to prevent someone else from restarting your account. Go to netflix.com/password and change your password. Be sure to check the box that says Sign out of all devices. This will change your current password and sign out any devices where your account is still signed in.

Why did my Netflix price change? Plans and pricing may change as we continue to improve our service and add more TV shows and movies. Adjustments can also happen in response to local market changes, such as changes to local taxes or inflation.

How will I be notified of a price change? If your price is changing, Netflix will send you an email with details about the price change one month before the billing date on which your price will increase. You can also review the payment history on your account to check your billing date and plan price. If you have a Netflix package add-on through a partner, your partner will send the price change notification.
What if I didn't see the email about the price change? If you did not see the email, please confirm that the email address associated with your account is correct.
Where can I compare Netflix plans and prices? You can compare our current plans and pricing and change plans at any time.
Can I keep the old plan price? Once a price change is announced, the new plan prices apply to everyone. You can change your plan at any time.

Why was I charged for a higher plan? If you or someone in your household upgraded your Netflix plan, your bill will reflect the higher plan price. This includes special sign-up or upgrade offers for a limited time. Once the offer ends, you may be charged the higher plan price. Check the email or text you received when the offer started for the original details.

How do I hide titles from my viewing history? When you hide titles from your viewing history, they won't appear in Netflix as a TV show or movie you've watched. They won't be used to make recommendations to you, unless you watch them again. They'll be removed from the Continue Watching row.

How to hide titles from your viewing history: From a web browser, go to your Account page. Select Profiles, then choose a profile. Open Viewing activity for that profile. On the Watching page, click the hide icon next to the episode or title you want to hide. If you hide an episode, you'll see the option to hide the entire series. To hide all of your viewing history, select the Hide all option at the bottom of the page and confirm. It can take up to 24 hours for a hidden title to be removed from all your devices. Titles can't be hidden if the Viewing activity page is reached from a Netflix Kids profile.

What should I do if I receive phishing or suspicious emails or texts claiming to be from Netflix? If you get an email or text message (SMS) asking for your Netflix account email, phone, password, or payment method, it probably didn't come from Netflix. Here’s how to identify and handle suspicious emails and texts to keep your account safe.

How to tell if an email or text is actually from Netflix: We’ll never ask you to share your personal information in a text or email, such as credit or debit card numbers, bank account details, or Netflix passwords. We’ll never ask for payment through a 3rd party vendor or website. If the text or email links to a URL that you don't recognize, don't tap or click it. If you already did, do not enter any information on the website that opened.
What should I do if I get a suspicious email or text? Scammers can’t get information from you unless you give it to them. So don’t click any links in the messages or reply to them.

For suspicious emails: Don’t click any of the links or open any of the attachments. Forward the email to phishing@netflix.com. If your email is rejected when you forward it, it means we have already received a copy of the phishing message. You don't need to do anything else except delete the email or message.

For suspicious text messages (SMS) on Android: Tap and hold the message that you want to forward. Tap Forward message, then tap the Forward arrow. Enter phishing@netflix.com, select the address, and tap Send. Finally, delete the message.

For suspicious text messages (SMS) on iPhone or iPad: Tap and hold the message that you want to forward. Tap More... and then the Forward arrow. Enter phishing@netflix.com, then tap Send. Delete the message.

What if I already clicked a link or shared personal information? Change your Netflix password to a strong, unique one. Update your password on any other websites or apps where you used the same email and password combination. Contact your financial institution if you entered any payment information. Forward the message to phishing@netflix.com as outlined above.

What if I see a website, app, or phone number pretending to be Netflix or Netflix Customer Service? Don’t click any links or provide information to the website or app. Send a copy of the phone number, link to the website, or app pretending to be from Netflix in an email to phishing@netflix.com.

What are the best ways to keep my information safe? Be cautious with emails or texts requesting personal information. Don't click a link if you're unsure; go directly to the official website. Never send personal or financial information via email. Check the sender’s address, hover over links to inspect the URL, and install anti-virus software to protect your devices and information.

How do I stop someone from using my Netflix account? If someone is using your account without permission, change your Netflix password to a unique one that’s not used for other websites or apps. Make sure it’s at least 8 characters long, using a combination of letters, numbers, and symbols with both upper and lower-case letters. Avoid easily guessed passwords like 'password,' '12345,' or any personal information like your name or birthday.

How do I sign out of devices I don't recognize? To sign out of devices you don't recognize, go to your Netflix account settings and choose 'Sign out of all devices.' This will remove all devices connected to your account. You will need to sign in again with your new password on any devices you wish to use.

What if I continue seeing suspicious account activity after changing my password? If you continue to see suspicious activity after resetting your password, or if you're unable to reset your password, please contact Netflix support for further assistance.

How can I make my Netflix account more secure? You can make your Netflix account more secure by using a password manager to keep track of unique passwords, and following other security steps provided in the 'How to keep your account secure' section on the Netflix help page.

How can I keep my Netflix account secure? To keep your account secure, use a password that’s unique to Netflix and not used anywhere else. Make sure the password is at least 8 characters long, with a mix of uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols. Avoid easily guessed passwords like 'password' or using personal information like your name or birthday.

What should I do if I use the same password for multiple services? If you use the same password for multiple services, it’s important to change it. If one account is compromised, attackers can use that password to access other accounts. Use a password manager to keep track of unique passwords for each service.

What kind of personal information should I keep safe? Netflix will never ask for your credit or debit card numbers, bank account details, or your Netflix password via text or email. Be cautious if anyone asks for this kind of information.

How do I sign out of unused or unrecognized devices? You can sign out of all devices or specific unused or unrecognized devices from the 'Manage Access & Devices' page. If you’re getting rid of a device, be sure to sign out of Netflix and any other accounts on that device.

What is phishing and how can I avoid it? Phishing is when someone pretends to be a trusted company, like Netflix, to steal your personal information. Avoid clicking on suspicious links in emails or texts. If you think you’ve received a phishing message or visited a fake website, refer to the 'Phishing or suspicious emails or texts claiming to be from Netflix' page for more details.

How do I report fraudulent activity on my account? If you notice suspicious or unrecognized charges or unauthorized account activity, contact Netflix immediately so we can investigate and help you resolve the issue.
Why should I add a phone number to my Netflix account? Adding a phone number helps you recover your Netflix password if you forget it. You can manage your phone number from your Account settings.

What can I do to keep my computer safe while using Netflix? To keep your computer safe, ensure it's free from malware and viruses. Watch out for signs like slow performance, pop-ups, and unexpected crashes. Use trusted anti-malware software and keep it up to date to protect your device.

How can I report a security flaw on Netflix? If you discover a security vulnerability on Netflix, report it through our bug bounty program. Do not disclose the vulnerability publicly until it’s fixed. We appreciate your help in making Netflix more secure.

I received a 'password reset required' email. What should I do? If you received a password reset email, click or tap the 'set a new password' button in the email. Alternatively, you can visit Netflix.com, select 'sign in,' and then choose 'Need help?' to reset your password directly.

What should my new Netflix password be? Your new password should be unique to Netflix and not used for other websites or apps. It should be at least 8 characters long and include a combination of uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols. Avoid using easily guessed passwords like 'password,' '12345,' or any personal information.
Can I use my old Netflix password when resetting it? No, you cannot use your old password. If you attempt to reset it to your old one, you'll get an error message.

How can I ensure my password is secure? Your password should be unique to Netflix and not used elsewhere. If you see a message saying 'This password is not secure or is too common' when resetting your password, it means the password you chose is on a list of compromised or commonly used passwords, and you’ll need to choose a different one.

How do I sign out of devices during the reset process? When resetting your password, choose the option to 'Sign out of all devices.' This will log out all devices connected to your Netflix account, and you'll need to sign in again with your new password.

How long will it take for my devices to be signed out? It can take up to 1 hour for all devices to be signed out. After that, you’ll need to sign in again using your new password for any devices you wish to use.
What if I use the same password for other websites? If you used the same password for other sites, it’s recommended to create unique passwords for each one to avoid potential security issues.
Why did Netflix reset my password? Netflix sometimes resets passwords due to data breaches at other companies, phishing attempts, suspicious account behavior, or malware attacks.

How can I manage the emails I receive from Netflix? To manage the emails you receive from Netflix, sign in to the Netflix profile you want to update, go to the Account page, select Profiles, choose your profile, and then select 'Notification settings.' From there, click 'Manage Emails' to toggle the types of emails you want to receive.

What email categories can I opt in or out of? You can opt in or out of several email categories, such as 'What you watch' (new seasons & episodes, Leaving Soon, Your Collections), 'Watch recommendations & more' (Top 10, New arrivals), 'Surveys & research invites,' 'Netflix Games' (releases, recommendations), 'Netflix.Shop & experiences,' 'Using the Netflix app,' 'Membership offers,' and 'Kids Activity Report' (if you have an active Kids profile).

How do I unsubscribe from all Netflix emails? To unsubscribe from all Netflix emails, you can go to the 'Manage Emails' section within your Notification settings and choose the option to unsubscribe from all.

How can I manage text messages from Netflix? To manage text messages from Netflix, go to your Account page, select Mobile phone in the Security section. From there, you can add, update, or remove a phone number. You can also opt out of text messages.
How do I stop receiving text messages from Netflix? To stop receiving all text messages from Netflix, reply with 'STOP' to any message you receive.
How do I get help via text from Netflix? To receive help from Netflix via text, reply with 'HELP' to any message, and you will be directed to the Netflix Help Center.

What are customer surveys from Netflix? Netflix occasionally sends surveys by email to gather feedback from members and former members. This feedback helps Netflix improve its product and content offerings. These surveys are sent via third-party providers and may include links to websites like netflixsurveys.com, previewclub.netflixsurveys.com, and more.

Can I stop receiving Netflix customer surveys? If you'd like to stop receiving these surveys, you can unsubscribe by selecting the 'unsubscribe' link in the emails or by visiting the Notification Settings page and toggling off 'Surveys & research invites.'
How can I report a phishing survey from Netflix? If you receive a survey invitation that you suspect is phishing, please forward the email to phishing@netflix.com.

What personal information does Netflix hold about me? Netflix holds various types of personal information related to your account, including your account information (email and phone number), plan selection, notification settings, privacy and data settings, payment and billing information, profile details, content interaction history, and access & device information. You can view and manage these details under your Account page, and some are specific to individual profiles.

How can I request a copy of my personal information from Netflix? If you'd like to request a copy of your personal information (known as a 'subject access request'), Netflix provides an option for this through their privacy policy or support. Please consult Netflix's Privacy and Data Settings for instructions on how to initiate this request.

What can I see about my Netflix account as the account owner? As the account owner, you can view details like account information (email, phone number), membership details, notification settings, privacy preferences, payment and billing information, profiles, content interaction history, and device access information for the last 90 days. This information is available on your Netflix Account page, with certain details accessible through individual profile settings.

How can I delete my Netflix account? To delete your Netflix account, refer to the article on Deleting your Netflix account for detailed instructions on how to close your account and remove all associated data.

How can I create, edit, or delete profiles on my Netflix account? To create, edit, or delete profiles, visit the 'How to create, edit or delete profiles' article, which provides step-by-step guidance on managing profiles within your account.

How can I hide titles from my viewing history? You can hide titles from your viewing history by following the instructions in the article 'How to hide titles from viewing history,' which guides you on how to remove unwanted titles from your account's history.

How can I remove a payment method from my Netflix account? To remove a payment method from your account, refer to the 'How to remove payment methods from your account' article, which outlines how to manage your payment information.

How can I add, change, or delete my phone number on Netflix? To add, change, or delete a phone number associated with your Netflix account, see the 'How to add, change or delete a phone number' article for detailed instructions.

What should I do if I want to remove my email address from a Netflix account? If your email address is associated with a Netflix account that you did not create and you wish to have your email address removed, contact privacy@netflix.com for assistance. For deleting an email address linked to a secondary profile, see the 'How to create, edit or delete profiles' article.

How long does Netflix retain my personal information? Netflix retains personal information as required or permitted by applicable laws, including for billing, record-keeping, and other necessary purposes. The retention period is explained further in the Netflix Privacy Statement. For more details, visit the Privacy and Security help page.

How can I delete my Netflix account? To delete your Netflix account, sign in to your account and go to the Security section on the Account page. You can request the deletion of your account using the web browser, Netflix app, or a Netflix game app. After confirming, Netflix will send an email confirmation of your request. If your membership is still active, your account will be canceled at the end of the current billing period and then permanently deleted.

Can I undo my Netflix account deletion request? Yes, you can undo your deletion request anytime before the end of your current billing period by going to the Account page. If you change your mind, you can reactivate your account before it is permanently deleted.

What happens after I delete my Netflix account? Once your account is permanently deleted, you will no longer have access to your account, preferences, or viewing history. To use Netflix again, you would need to sign up for a new account.

Can I delete my account if I am a current Netflix member? If you're a current member, you can cancel your membership first, either through the Account page or by contacting your third-party billing provider. After canceling, you can contact privacy@netflix.com to request the deletion of your account.

How can I delete my Netflix account if I’m a former member? Former members can request the earlier deletion of their Netflix account by contacting privacy@netflix.com from the email address associated with their account.

Can I delete my Netflix account using a mobile or game app? Yes, you can delete your account from the Netflix mobile app or Netflix game app by going to the Account settings and following the instructions to confirm the deletion.

What if I didn’t complete my Netflix sign-up? If you started the sign-up process but did not complete it, contact privacy@netflix.com from the email address you used to start the sign-up to request the deletion of your entered information.

How can I access and update information associated with my Netflix account? To access and update information on your Netflix account, log in through a browser and go to the Account page. From there, you can view and update your account information such as email, phone number, plan selection, payment details, notification settings, and more.

What types of information can I view on the Netflix Account page? On the Account page, you can access various types of information such as account information (email and phone), notification settings, privacy and data preferences, payment and billing details, profiles, content interaction history, and access & device information.

Can I update my personal information on the Netflix Account page? Yes, you can update your email address, method of payment, phone number, password, notification settings, and even hide titles from your viewing activity on the Account page.

What should I do if I want to change my name, email address, payment method, or password? If you wish to change your name, email address, payment method, or password, you can contact Customer Service. You will need to go through a verification process before they assist you with any changes.

Can I access my viewing activity and device details? Yes, on the Account page, you can review your content interaction history, which includes a history of your viewing activity and interactions with titles. You can also see details about devices that have been active on your account within the last 90 days.

How can I update my notification settings on Netflix? To update your notification preferences, go to the Account page, select your profile, and then go to the Notification settings section where you can adjust the types of Netflix communications you wish to receive.

How do I hide titles from my viewing history? To hide titles from your viewing activity, visit the Account page, go to Viewing activity, and select the titles you want to hide. You can also hide all of your viewing history from this page.

How can I stop certain uses of my personal information on Netflix? You can control how Netflix uses your personal information through various account settings. For example, you can opt-out of behavioral advertising, manage matched identifier communications, and control device data collection by adjusting settings in the 'Privacy and Data Settings' section of your Account page.

How can I opt-out of behavioral advertising on Netflix? To opt-out of behavioral advertising, sign in to your Netflix account, go to the 'Privacy and Data Settings' section under the 'Account' menu, and toggle off the 'Behavioral Advertising' option for the relevant profile. This will stop Netflix from showing ads based on your activities across third-party services, but you will still see ads.

What are matched identifier communications on Netflix, and how can I stop them? Matched identifier communications allow Netflix to use third-party services for online marketing by matching de-identified contact information (like your email) with third-party databases. To opt-out, go to the 'Privacy and Data Settings' section under your Account settings and turn off 'Matched Identifier Communications'.

How does Netflix determine if a device is part of my Netflix Household? Netflix uses information like IP addresses and device IDs to determine if a device is part of your Netflix Household. If you want to stop this data collection on a specific device, simply sign out of that device.

What choices do I have if I'm not signed in to my Netflix account? If you're not signed in to Netflix, the data we collect is limited. However, your device settings (e.g., cookies, tracking) may offer controls over what information Netflix collects. You can manage cookies, resettable device identifiers, and control other online tracking settings specific to your device.

How do I manage cookies and tracking on Netflix's website? To control cookies on Netflix's website, you can click the relevant options about cookies when visiting the site. Note that Netflix does not respond to web browser 'do not track' signals. You can adjust cookie settings based on your browser or device and also manage advertising preferences in the 'Privacy and Data Settings' section of your Account page.

Can I control resettable device identifiers on Netflix? Yes, you can control resettable device identifiers used by Netflix for marketing and advertising. This setting can be adjusted through your device's 'privacy' or 'ads' settings. If you are eligible for behavioral advertising, this control will stop marketing based on that identifier.

What is the Digital Advertising Alliance (DAA) opt-out, and how does it work on Netflix? The DAA opt-out lets you control how third parties use your information for behavioral advertising across different services. You can opt-out by visiting the Digital Advertising Alliance website specific to your region (e.g., DAA for the U.S., EDAA for Europe, and DAAC for Canada).

How do I manage email preferences on Netflix? To manage the emails you receive from Netflix, go to the 'Notification Settings' section of your Account page. You can toggle preferences for different types of communication, such as recommendations, surveys, and Netflix promotions.

How do I stop receiving text messages from Netflix? To stop receiving text messages, go to your Account page, select 'Security', then 'Mobile phone'. You can remove your phone number by selecting 'Delete Phone Number'. Note that you may still receive essential text messages like password resets even after opting out.

How can I turn off push notifications from Netflix? To stop receiving push notifications, go to your mobile device's settings and disable notifications for the Netflix app, or you can change notification settings in the 'Account' section of the Netflix website for each profile.

How does Netflix's recommendations system work? Netflix's recommendations system estimates the likelihood that you'll enjoy a particular title based on factors like your viewing history, how you rate content, the preferences of similar members, and details about the titles (like genre and actors). We also consider factors like the time of day you're watching, your preferred language, and the devices you're using.

What factors influence Netflix's recommendations? Our recommendations system considers several factors, such as your interactions with Netflix (viewing history, ratings), the preferences of similar members, details of the titles (genre, actors, release year), and additional inputs like the time of day, preferred language, and devices you're using.

Does Netflix use demographic information for recommendations? No, Netflix's recommendations system does not include demographic information such as age or gender. We focus on your viewing behavior, preferences, and other signals to personalize recommendations.

What should I do if I'm not seeing the shows or movies I want? If you're not seeing something you'd like to watch, you can always use the search feature. The top search results are based on your preferences, the actions of other users, and predictions of what you might enjoy based on your past behavior.

How does Netflix 'jump start' the recommendations system when I create an account? When you first create your Netflix account or add a new profile, Netflix may ask you to choose a few titles you like. These selections help us 'jump start' the recommendations system, giving it a better idea of your tastes. If you skip this step, Netflix will start with a diverse set of popular titles for you.

What happens once I start engaging with titles on Netflix? Once you begin interacting with content, your engagement (such as what you watch and how you rate titles) will become more important in shaping your recommendations. This will gradually replace any initial preferences you provided at sign-up.

How does Netflix organize and rank titles on the homepage? Netflix ranks titles on your homepage based on what you are most likely to enjoy. In each row, we personalize which titles appear and the order in which they appear, with the most recommended titles shown first. For languages like Arabic or Hebrew, rows are displayed right to left.

How does Netflix improve its recommendations over time? Netflix continually updates its recommendations system based on your feedback from each visit—like which titles you start watching, finish, or rate highly. This feedback helps refine the algorithms, ensuring that the system presents fresh, relevant recommendations to you over time.

What is the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)? The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) is a law in the European Union that governs how companies can use personal information. It came into effect on 25 May 2018 and grants individuals in the European Economic Area (EEA) specific rights regarding their personal information.

What rights do I have under the GDPR? The GDPR provides several rights, including: the right to access your personal information, the right to update or rectify your personal information, the right to delete or erase your personal information in certain circumstances, the right to object to or restrict the processing of your personal information, and the right to request a copy of your personal information.

How can I access my personal information under the GDPR? Much of the personal information that Netflix stores about its members is accessible by logging into your account and clicking on the 'Account' option. You can review and manage your information through this page.

Can I update my personal information on Netflix under the GDPR? Yes, you can easily update your personal information by logging into your account and clicking on the 'Account' option in a browser. This allows you to change your details as needed.

Can I request to delete or remove my personal information? Yes, under the GDPR, you have the right to request the deletion (or erasure) of your personal information in certain circumstances. You can learn more about how to delete and remove your information by referring to Netflix’s guidelines.

How can I object to or restrict the processing of my personal information? If you wish to object to or restrict the processing of your personal information, you can do so in accordance with Netflix’s privacy guidelines. This may include withdrawing your consent if that was the basis for processing your information.

How can I request a copy of my personal information? You have the right to request a copy of your personal information under the GDPR. You can find more details on how to request this information on Netflix's help pages.

Can I file a complaint about Netflix’s data practices under the GDPR? Yes, you have the right to complain to a data protection authority about Netflix’s collection and use of your personal information if you believe it does not comply with the GDPR.

How do I make a request or contact Netflix about data protection? If you wish to make any requests or have questions about Netflix's privacy practices, you can contact the Data Protection Officer/Privacy Office at privacy@netflix.com. Netflix responds to all such requests in accordance with applicable data protection laws.

What devices can I use to watch Netflix? You can watch Netflix on a wide range of supported devices, including smart TVs, phones, tablets, streaming players, game consoles, and computers. As long as the device connects to the internet and supports the Netflix app, you can stream Netflix content.

What TVs and TV streaming devices support Netflix? Many smart TVs and TV streaming devices come with the Netflix app. Some supported brands include Amazon Fire TV/Stick, Apple TV, Chromecast, Hisense, LG, Panasonic, Philips, Roku TV/Stick, Samsung, Sharp, Sony, TCL, Vestel, Vizio, and Toshiba. Please note that Netflix may no longer be available on some devices made before 2015.

How do I watch Netflix on a smart TV? To get started, use the Netflix app that comes pre-installed on most supported smart TVs. If you don't see the app, you can search for your TV's model or manufacturer in the Netflix Help Center for more information.

Which mobile phones and tablets can I use to watch Netflix? You can watch Netflix on supported Android phones or tablets, iPhones, iPads, Amazon Fire tablets, and Windows tablets. To get started, download the Netflix app from the respective app store for your device.

Can I watch Netflix on my computer? Yes, you can watch Netflix on Mac, Windows, or Chromebook computers. To get started, go to netflix.com using a supported web browser, or download the Netflix app for Windows. Make sure your computer and browser meet the system requirements.

Can I watch Netflix on a cable box? Yes, many internet, cable, and pay TV providers offer the Netflix app on their devices. To see if Netflix is available on your cable box, search for your provider's name in the Netflix Help Center or contact your provider for more details.

Which video game consoles and AR/VR devices support Netflix? You can watch Netflix on popular game consoles such as Sony PlayStation and Microsoft Xbox consoles. Netflix is also supported on AR/VR devices like the Apple Vision Pro and Meta Quest headsets.
